question: Base of a triangle is 9 and height is 5. Base of another triangle is 10 and height is 6. Find the ratio of areas of these triangles.
answer:
1Show Solution
Let ABC and PQR be two right triangles with AD ‚üā BC and PS ‚üā QR.
BC = 9, AD = 5, PS = 6 and QR = 10.
The ratio of areas of two triangles is equal to the ratio of the products of their bases and corresponding heights.
("A"(Δ"ABC"))/("A"(Δ"PQR")) = ("AD" √ó "BC")/("PS" √ó "QR")
‚ąī ("A"(Δ"ABC"))/("A"(Δ"PQR")) = (5 √ó 9)/(6 √ó 10)
‚ąī ("A"(Δ"ABC"))/("A"(Δ"PQR")) = 3/4
Hence, Ratio of Area of ‚Ė≥ABC : Area of ‚Ė≥PQR = 3 : 4.
subject: Mathmetics

question: If ‚ąÜABC ~ ‚ąÜPQR, A (‚ąÜABC) = 80, A (‚ąÜPQR) = 125, then fill in the blanks. A ( őĒ A B C ) A ( őĒ . . . . ) = 80 125 ‚ąī A B P Q = . . . . . . . . . . . .

\[ \Rightarrow \frac{AB}{PQ} = \frac{4}{5}\]

\[\frac{A\left( ‚ąÜ ABC \right)}{A\left( ‚ąÜ PQR \right)} = \frac{80}{125} and \frac{AB}{PQ} = \frac{4}{5}\]
